danger_signals.visual_form: Cascading enemy formations entering from screen edges
danger_signals.meaning: Incoming threat requiring evasion or engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Large scale adversary filling significant screen space, distinct visual design from standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Major combat encounter; stage progression checkpoint

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinctive large enemy sprite with architectural/stone-like aesthetic
Screen shifts vertically to expose new enemy waves in upper/lower play field areas
danger_signals.meaning: Hidden threats accessible through positional awareness; environmental danger variability

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items descending from defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced weaponry or defensive capability; progression reward
Temporary damage mitigation; defensive safety state

safety_signals.visual_form: Orbiting companion vessels circling player ship
safety_signals.meaning: Increased firepower and screen coverage; safety multiplier

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple missile and laser projectile types visually distinct in firing pattern
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical flexibility; adaptation safety through loadout selection

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Enemy creatures and mechanical units (Gargoyle Diver example)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Stage-specific enemy designs representing Orn forces across different environments
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stage environments designed as Orn-controlled territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player assumes role as fighter pilot; narrative context two years after Thunder Force III events
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player selects from available stage routes (first four stages in any order); agency in approach strategy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Continuous enemy waves, boss encounters, multi-stage campaign structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player navigates through ten stages total with mandatory combat encounters and optional vertical exploration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage environments represent Orn-held locations; distinct visual design per stage indicating conquered territory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player progresses through enemy-controlled space; stages present escalating difficulty and varied threat compositions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Acquisition of Thunder Sword weapon halfway through campaign; power progression visual feedback
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unlocked capability requiring two satellite ships; dramatic offensive capability surge

environmental_storytelling.example: Gargoyle Diver first boss suggests underground/architectural Orn base; enemy design reflects location thematic
Hidden enemy waves and power-ups in upper/lower play field areas suggest layered Orn defenses; exploration rewards spatial awareness
Named bosses (Gargoyle Diver) provide entity identity within faction; personal antagonist perception
Progressive weapon unlocks communicate human technological advancement; Thunder Sword as late-game acquisition suggests humanity's escalating capability
First four stage selectable in any order suggests distributed Orn Empire presence; multiple viable strategic approaches

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responsive speed adjustment; weapon cycling; vertical movement for environmental exploration; defensive positioning
Formation-based attack waves; predictable spawn patterns from screen edges; death drops power-ups
Large scale presence; stage-blocking positioning; multi-phase combat; demanding sustained player engagement
Distributed across ten stage environments; escalating difficulty progression; varied enemy types per region

notes: Thunder Force IV communicates world-state primarily through combat intensity, enemy composition variation, and progressive power unlocks rather than explicit faction visual identifiers (colors, symbols). The Orn Empire exists as an implied antagonist force manifested through enemy design diversity and stage environments. The player's progression from stage selection freedom to Thunder Sword acquisition narratively suggests humanity's transition from defensive positioning to offensive capability. The game uses vertical scrolling as a core environmental storytelling mechanic—hidden threats and rewards communicate that the Orn Empire's presence extends beyond obvious threats, rewarding spatial exploration.
